items.title: ‘I’m living year to year now’: Neymar says he may retire by end of 2026
items.summary: Santos player has struggled with injury in recent seasons Brazilian forward remains doubtful for this year’s World Cup Brazil striker Neymar, who extended his contract with his boyhood club Santos last month, said that he may retire by the end of the year. The 34-year-old forward returned to Santos in January 2025 and played a key role in their survival in the Brazilian top flight, scoring five times in their last five matches. Continue reading...

items.title: Sources: Prestianni told UEFA he used anti-gay slur
items.summary: Gianluca Prestianni has already given evidence in UEFA's investigation into his alleged racist abuse of Vinícius Júnior, sources told ESPN.

items.title: IOC to probe Infantino conduct after Trump event
items.summary: After FIFA leader Gianni Infantino took part in U.S. President Donald Trump's Board of Peace launch, IOC President Kirsty Coventry said the Olympic body will "look into" his conduct.

items.title: Arteta rejects 'bottlers' tag amid Arsenal wobble
items.summary: Mikel Arteta insisted the word "bottlers" is not part of his vocabulary as he denied Arsenal are blowing another chance to win the Premier League.

items.title: MLS team-by-team preview: Why is 2026 your team's year?
items.summary: With the 2026 MLS season kicking off Saturday, ESPN breaks down where the league's 30 clubs stand and how they've prepped for a run at MLS Cup.

items.title: Can Messi reach Ronaldo's target of 1,000 goals?
items.summary: Lionel Messi begins the 2026 MLS season on the verge of another huge milestone: his 900th goal.

items.title: Boxing legend Mayweather coming out of retirement
items.summary: Floyd Mayweather, who will turn 49 later this month, has announced he is coming out of retirement after his fight with Mike Tyson in April.

items.title: De Decker sets up Littler encounter with Ratajski to face Humphries in Poland
items.summary: Belgian No 1 Mike De Decker set up blockbuster encounter against world No 1 Luke Littler at the SUPERBET Poland Darts Masters as Krzysztof Ratajski delivered on home soil.

items.title: McTominay krijgt schitterend eerbetoon en klaagt over 'soft' geworden voetbal
items.summary: Wie in Glasgow langs Hampden Park loopt, komt rondom het nationale stadion een indrukwekkende muurschildering tegen. Scott McTominay siert een omliggend gebouw, in de acrobatische pose waarin hij in november scoorde voor de nationale ploeg.

items.title: Relegated in February? Why Sheff Wed are on brink of history
items.summary: Sheffield Wednesday are on the brink of becoming the first team in English Football League history to be relegated in February.

items.title: Benn makes big career switch for 'legacy fights'
items.summary: Conor Benn has signed to Dana White and Zuffa Boxing, saying he wants "legacy fights" and "the biggest stages".

items.title: Mikel Arteta rules out ‘bottlers’ jibe but accepts Arsenal must take title criticism
items.summary: ‘It’s not part of my vocabulary. I wouldn’t use that word’ Arsenal visit Tottenham in Premier League on Sunday Mikel Arteta has insisted the word “bottlers” is not in his vocabulary and that Arsenal must take criticism “on the chin” after surrendering a 2-0 lead against the bottom side, Wolves, in midweek. With Manchester City facing Newcastle on Saturday night, the Premier League leaders could have their advantage over Pep Guardiola’s side cut to two points by the time they play at Tottenham on Sunday. Continue reading...

items.title: Tudor promises Spurs will avoid relegation but accepts club are in ‘emergency situation’
items.summary: New coach starts tenure against Arsenal on Sunday Tudor insists ‘we have enormous quality in the squad’ Igor Tudor has said Tottenham are in “an emergency situation” but issued a guarantee that his new team would escape relegation. Tudor is a veteran of short-term appointments and has performed rescue jobs at Udinese, Verona and Juventus. He offered a curt “100%” in response to questions on his confidence in Spurs’s survival. The Croat’s tenure begins on Sunday with a north London derby at home to the wobbling Premier League leaders, though a lengthy injury list leaves him with only 13 fit senior players. Continue reading...

items.title: ‘I won the league with Liverpool’: Arne Slot responds to Wayne Rooney jibe
items.summary: Rooney claimed coach lacked ‘aura’ to manage club ‘Jürgen Klopp and me both won the league’ Arne Slot has responded to Wayne Rooney’s claim that the Liverpool head coach does not have the “aura” to manage the club by pointing to his Premier League title success last season. Rooney questioned Slot’s suitability for the job, telling The Overlap: “I have met him a few times. He was a nice fella but I just don’t think, for Liverpool, he’s got that aura about him. Maybe this is because you have just come off the back of Jürgen Klopp and it’s going to be difficult for anyone to have that, but I just don’t think there’s that aura about him.” Continue reading...

items.title: Technisch directeur Corinthians bespreekt contractsituatie Memphis
items.summary: Een dag nadat Memphis Depay zich uitsprak over zijn toekomst bij Corinthians , is ook de technisch directeur van de club aan het woord gekomen. In gesprek met de Braziliaanse afdeling van ESPN sprak Marcelo Paz de verwachting uit dat Memphis in Brazilië wil blijven.

items.title: García haalt vrije dagen weg bij spelers Jong Ajax: 'Zijn al 2,5 week op de club'
items.summary: Oscar García boekte vrijdagavond zijn eerste zege met Jong Ajax . Nadat zijn debuut moeizaam verloren ging tegen SC Cambuur ( 0-1 ), stopten de Amsterdammers periodekoploper FC Dordrecht.

items.title: Bradford blow Catalans away to end 12-year wait for Super League win
items.summary: Bradford enjoyed a dream Betfred Super League homecoming at Odsal as they beat Catalans Dragons 28-10 for their first top-flight victory in 12 years.

items.title: Papers: Carrick 'firmly in the running' for Man Utd job
items.summary: The top stories and transfer rumours from Saturday's newspapers...

items.title: Debuut Beye bij Marseille eindigt in Bretonse blamage
items.summary: Vrijdagavond om 20.00 uur staan Fortuna Sittard en Excelsior tegenover elkaar. Dat zijn de nummers elf en twaalf van de Eredivisie. Beide ploegen hebben na 23 duels evenveel punten verzameld (26). Volg het duel in deze VI Live.

items.title: Excelsior schippert tussen woede en berusting over omstreden penalty
items.summary: Excelsior verloor vrijdagavond op bezoek bij Fortuna Sittard. Het Kralingse onheil werd ingeluid door een penalty die vlak voor rust werd toegekend wegens hands van Casper Widell. Widell was het daar niet mee eens, zijn trainer Ruben den Uil reageerde ontspannen. De arbiter hield overigens voet bij stuk.
